hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Ace, 3, 2. Your opponent checked. You bet. Your opponent called.
River — 9. Your opponent led out with a bet.

wrongReasons: 4 10 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that 4 10 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
5 9 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only high card, 6 J wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 2

Your opponent flopped a pair but played it passively — calling the flop and checking the turn — before leading the river to extract thin value. The check-call-check-lead line is a tell for a medium-strength made hand.
